---
title: "Protocols"
---

Protocol calculators are **Shiny documents** — they live in `protocols/_shiny/`
and are served individually, not as part of the notebook site.
They cannot be included in a static website, so they are not browsable here.

## How to open a protocol calculator

Protocol calculators must be served from **inside** `protocols/_shiny/` —
running `quarto serve` from the repo root fails because the parent `_quarto.yml`
defines a website project, which is incompatible with Shiny.

```bash
cd protocols/_shiny

quarto serve HiC_protocol.qmd      # in situ Hi-C
quarto serve CnR_protocol.qmd      # CUT&RUN
quarto serve ENS_2D_protocol.qmd   # 2D ENS differentiation

# When done: Ctrl+C, then cd ../.. to return to the repo root
```

Each opens in your browser. Configure your inputs in the sidebar, then
**File → Print → Save as PDF** for your bench copy.

## Available protocols

| Protocol | File | Type | Source |
|----------|------|------|--------|
| In situ Hi-C | `protocols/_shiny/HiC_protocol.qmd` | Chromatin conformation | Phanstiel lab · Rao et al. 2014 |
| CUT&RUN | `protocols/_shiny/CnR_protocol.qmd` | Chromatin profiling | Phanstiel lab · Skene et al. 2018 |
| 2D ENS differentiation | `protocols/_shiny/ENS_2D_protocol.qmd` | Cell biology | Barber, Studer & Fattahi 2019 |

## Adding a new protocol

Give Claude the source (PDF, `.docx`, or paste the methods section) and say
**"make a QMD for this protocol."**
Output goes in `protocols/_shiny/`. Then add a row to the table above.

## Why the separation?

Quarto website projects render everything to static HTML.
Shiny documents require a live R process and can't be static.
They coexist in the same repo but are used differently:

- **Notebook site** (`quarto render` from project root) — static, browsable, searchable
- **Protocol calculators** (`cd protocols/_shiny && quarto serve X.qmd`) — interactive, run on demand
